updates.title: Claude just added Fable 5 back to your subscription, but there's a catch
updates.summary: Anthropic has re-added Claude Fable 5 to all paid Claude plans (Pro, Max, Team, select Enterprise) following the lifting of US export controls on June 30, 2026 — but the inclusion is temporary. Subscribers can use Fable 5 for up to 50% of their weekly usage limit through July 7, after which the model will only be available via usage credits. The export controls, which forced an immediate global suspension in mid-June because Anthropic had no real-time nationality-verification mechanism, have now been fully lifted.
updates.accuracy_notes: Publication date confirmed as July 4, 2026 (article appeared as '2 hours ago' in search metadata). Core facts corroborated by Anthropic's own newsroom post (anthropic.com/news/redeploying-fable-5), MacRumors, and BleepingComputer. The July 7 cut-off date and 50%-of-weekly-limit cap are verified via the official Claude X/Twitter account post. No red flags.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.200–2.1.201 Released: Manual Permission Mode, AskUserQuestion Overhaul, and 15+ Bug Fixes
updates.summary: Anthropic shipped two Claude Code releases on July 3, 2026. v2.1.201 patches Claude Sonnet 5 sessions so harness reminders no longer inject into the mid-conversation system role. v2.1.200 introduces a renamed 'Manual' default permission mode (replacing 'default') and changes AskUserQuestion dialogs to not auto-continue — users must now opt into idle timeouts via /config. The release also fixes 15+ bugs covering background-session reliability, crash-on-startup edge cases, subagent rate-limit handling, plugin loading from git worktrees, screen-reader accessibility, voice dictation false errors, and tmux 3.4+ rendering flicker.
updates.accuracy_notes: Version numbers, dates, and all bullet points verified directly from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog (HTTP 200 response). No interpretation required — this is primary-source Anthropic documentation. Very high confidence.

updates.title: Claude Enterprise Gets Richer Admin Analytics, Model-Level Entitlements, and Spend Alerts
updates.summary: Anthropic rolled out a major upgrade to Claude Enterprise's admin console on July 3, 2026. Admins now see usage and cost breakdowns by SCIM group and individual user, with productivity outputs (artifacts created, files edited, skills and connectors used) displayed alongside their cost. New model-level entitlements let admins set defaults and hard limits per group, and configurable spend-threshold alerts prevent surprise overages. The same metrics are exposed via an Analytics API for integration with existing finance and IT reporting systems. Claude Code gets two new admin-console tabs showing active developers, session counts, and top commands across the org.
updates.accuracy_notes: Update date of July 3, 2026 confirmed via multiple search results referencing the releasebot.io Anthropic changelog. Feature details corroborated by the Claude Enterprise Analytics API support article (support.claude.com) and langwatch.ai breakdown. Blog URL verified as a real Anthropic page (claude.com/blog). High confidence — details are consistent across independent sources.
